summary: Large grzebes stage decomp/matching PR, initially disclosed as mostly AI-generated. It replaced many stubs in src/melee/gr/grzebes.c with typed C for Zebes stage object setup/update, acid/bubble/yakumono behavior, material callbacks, and zako-generator hooks, plus supporting Ground gv layouts, prototypes, and symbol fixes. decomp.dev reported +5868 matched code bytes and +800 matched data bytes, with 15 new full matches and 17 improved unmatched items.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: 2088 additions replacing many grZebes stubs with C bodies, local structs, constants, BSS/static state, acid/bubble logic, material callbacks, and collision/zako updates.
Extended Zebes Ground gv layouts: refined grZebes_GroundVars fields and added grZebes_GroundVars3/4/5 union members with offset-annotated fields.
Added precise prototypes for newly decompiled/exported grZebes functions, including Vec3*, Ground_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, and callback signatures.
Made grMaterial_801C8D44 non-static so grzebes.c could call it.
Added grMaterial_801C8D44 prototype with Item_GObj/Ground/Vec3/HSD_GObj/f32 callback types.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for grZakoGenerator_801CA43C with s32(void*, HSD_JObj*, f32).
Updated grZe data/sdata2 symbols, including grZe_callbacks and several local @nnn constants instead of uncertain global grZe_ address names.

what_changed: Implemented or substantially filled grZebes functions including grZebes_801D8558, 801D8644, 801D881C, 801D9100, 801D925C, 801D9410, 801D9508, 801D95B8, 801D9798, 801D99E0, 801D9F84, 801DA0C4, 801DA254, 801DA3F4, 801DA528, 801DAA08, 801DAE70, 801DB088, 801DB3CC, 801DBB60, 801DC260, 801DC408, 801DC744, 801DC9DC, and 801DCBFC.

Added feature structs in grzebes.c such as grZe_ColorEntry, grZe_AcidLevelEntry, grZe_YakumonoParam, grZe_BubbleEntry, grZe_BubbleConfig, and grZe_AcidState to make the acid/bubble/state-machine code addressable through typed fields instead of only raw offsets.
Added StageCallbacks grZe_callbacks and used it in grZebes_801D8558 for Ground_801C14D0 creation, GX link setup, callback dispatch, and an in-place OSReport fallback when a gobj cannot be obtained.

Introduced static/BSS state for Zebes bubble positions and entries, plus const bubble config grZe_803B8044 and helper routines for bubble spawning, updates, material envelope sizing, and collision surface updates.
Expanded grZebes header prototypes and Ground union layouts so the new decomp code could use stage-specific gv.zebes, gv.zebes2, gv.zebes3, gv.zebes4, and gv.zebes5 fields.
Exposed grMaterial_801C8D44 and typed grZakoGenerator_801CA43C so grzebes.c could stop relying on UNK_T/UNK_PARAMS for those interactions.
Adjusted GALE01 symbols to split/rename data and sdata2 symbols, notably naming grZe_callbacks and demoting many uncertain float/double constants to local @ labels.
smart_moves: Followed review guidance by using OSReport strings/arguments in place instead of preserving a separate named static format string; the final grZebes_801D8558 call mirrors the reviewed pattern.
Avoided over-naming uncertain constants: symbols.txt converts many grZe_804DB0xx sdata2 globals into local @186/@187/etc labels, reducing false confidence in data ordering and names.

Improved callback signatures rather than leaving UNK_T/UNK_PARAMS: grMaterial_801C8D44 and grZakoGenerator_801CA43C now expose the exact Item_GObj/Ground/Vec3/HSD_GObj/f32-style interfaces needed by the stage code.
Used stage-specific structs and offset comments for the large Ground gv union instead of scattering anonymous casts everywhere.
Applied reviewer advice to inline small Vec3 initializers such as pos1 and pos2 in grZebes_801D925C rather than adding unnecessary statics.
Let decomp.dev guide scope: the PR locked in several complete matches while also landing partial improvements for larger routines instead of pretending the whole file was fully matched.
decomp_lessons: For partially matched files, do not leave speculative static symbol ordering or names just because the current C needs constants; HSD_ASSERT strings and later matches may reveal the real names.
OSReport/HSD_ASSERT literals can often be matched in-place. Check nearby stage files, such as the grlast.c example cited by review, before creating named data objects.
Ground stage routines often need accurate gv union layouts before the code becomes readable or matchable; signedness changes such as u16 to s16 can matter.
Callback-heavy material/zako systems should be decompiled by first typing the callback prototypes, then threading those types through headers and callers.
Match-oriented C may require PAD_STACK, UNUSED padding, explicit casts, and non-idiomatic control flow; keep these tactics evidence-based and clean up names/data only when proven.
When AI or permuter output produces a match, still review for false statics, accidental global names, and opportunities to express literals/temporaries in compiler-friendly local form.
naming_conventions: Use grZebes_ for functions in src/melee/gr/grzebes.c and grZe_ for Zebes data/config symbols.
Name the stage callback table grZe_callbacks when the .data object is clearly a StageCallbacks array.
Use feature-based local struct names when behavior is identifiable, such as grZe_AcidLevelEntry, grZe_BubbleEntry, grZe_BubbleConfig, grZe_AcidState, and grZe_YakumonoParam.
Keep unresolved field names as offset-style xC4/xD8/xA0_entries with comments until semantics are known.
Use local @nnn symbols for compiler constants in symbols.txt when there is no evidence for a global grZe_ address-style name.
Prefer precise function prototypes in headers over UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once argument and return types are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used in-place OSReport with literal file name and line number in grZebes_801D8558 to avoid extra data and match the expected pattern.
Used inline Vec3 local initializers in grZebes_801D925C so the compiler treats them as constants without separate static declarations.
Adjusted symbol scope and sizes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, especially .data and .sdata2 local @ constants, to align data with compiler output.
Used PAD_STACK and UNUSED stack bytes in several routines to preserve stack layout where needed.
Used typed stage state structs and explicit casts for HSD_GObj, Ground_GObj, Item_GObj, HSD_JObj, and HSD_LObj interactions.
Matched math-heavy routines with explicit float/double constants, sqrtf from MSL/math_ppc.h, and HSD_Randf/HSD_Randi random range expressions.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether OSReport-related statics could be fixed to be used in-place, pointing to grlast.c as an example.
review_feedback.outcome: Final diff uses an in-place OSReport call in grZebes_801D8558.

review_feedback.feedback: Recommended removing the rest of speculative statics until the file is correctly matched, warning that otherwise it gives a false impression of ordering; HSD_ASSERT may reveal true temp names such as map_a_gobj.
review_feedback.outcome: Symbols were adjusted toward local @ constants and the author asked how to handle statics being used.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the revised handling of still-used statics was okay.
review_feedback.outcome: PsiLupan replied that it looked a lot better.

review_feedback.feedback: Suggested writing Vec3 temporaries directly as pos1 = { 24.1f, -4.6f, 0.0f }; and pos2 = { 24.05f, 2.2f, 0.0f }; so they are treated as const without static declarations.
review_feedback.outcome: Final grZebes_801D925C shows local Vec3 pos1 and pos2 initializers.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for grZebes_801D881C, grZebes_801DB3CC, grZebes_801DBB60, and grZebes_801D99E0 to see how the remaining partial matches were finalized.
Compare grzebes OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage with grlast.c for reusable in-place literal patterns.
Search for grZe_804D6990 and grZe_YakumonoParam to verify whether field names became semantic after more matching.
Find other stage files that expose grMaterial_801C8D44 or type grZakoGenerator_801CA43C to confirm callback signature reuse.
Review symbols.txt local @ constants around grZe_804DB088 through grZe_804DB120 before renaming any sdata2 values.
